MDR Rate (%) — All
About this map. This is the spatiotemporal evolution view of antimicrobial resistance across countries from 2004 to 2024 for nosocomial neuroinvasive bacterial pathogens. Select an organism and press Play to animate year by year and observe how resistance intensifies, spreads, or recedes over two decades.

Resistance type (green dropdown): choose between MDR (multi-drug resistant - non-susceptible to ≥3 antibiotic classes), XDR (extensively drug resistant - susceptible to ≤2 classes only), or PDR (pan-drug resistant - non-susceptible to all tested classes). All definitions follow Magiorakos et al. (CMI 2012; 18:268–281) using R + I = non-susceptible.

Age category (purple dropdown): All = Pediatric + Elderly pooled; Pediatric = 0–17 years; Elderly = 61+ years. Only country–year–organism–age combinations with ≥10 isolates are shown (WHO GLASS 2023 minimum threshold).

Controls: Organism (amber) = one of 13 WHO priority nosocomial pathogens.  |  Year (blue) = jump to any year 2004–2024.  |  Resistance Type (green) = MDR / XDR / PDR.  |  Age Category (purple) = All / Pediatric / Elderly.  |  Play / Pause = animate the map automatically. Hover any coloured country for detailed statistics.

Data source: Pfizer ATLAS Surveillance Programme 2004–2024. MDR definition: Magiorakos et al. CMI 2012. Income classification: World Bank Country & Lending Groups FY2026.
